ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Cakes) is the closest defensible parent category; no specific code exists for gula melaka or palm sugar cake.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard slice of Gula Melaka Cake weighs approximately 120 g, representing a single dessert portion as typically served at a café or restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Chocolate cake with icing, commercially prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (adapted as closest analogue for layered fudge cake); cross-referenced with USDA Foundation Foods butter cake components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct nutritional entry for Gula Melaka Cake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are estimated by adapting USDA SR Legacy entries for layered butter/fudge cake (NDB 18139 — cake, chocolate with icing) and plain butter cake components, adjusted for the higher palm sugar (gula melaka) content which contributes additional sucrose and trace minerals. Gula melaka has a similar caloric density to brown sugar (~380 kcal/100 g) but a slightly lower GI. Macros reflect a typical moist layered cake with fudge filling. All values are estimates and should be replaced with laboratory analysis when available.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of palm sugar and analogous baked cake products — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Gula Melaka Cake. GI estimated at 55 based on: (1) plain sponge/butter cake GI values of 46–54 reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international tables; (2) gula melaka (coconut palm sugar) has a published GI of approximately 35–54 (lower than refined sucrose GI ~65), which moderates the overall cake GI slightly relative to a sucrose-based cake; (3) the fudge filling and fat content further slow gastric emptying. A conservative estimate of GI 55 (upper bound of low category) is used. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 62.4) / 100 = 34.3, where carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 52.0 × 1.2 = 62.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~210 mg/100 g) and high saturated fat
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a standard slice, as a full 120 g serving contributes approximately 252 mg sodium and significant saturated fat, both of which can elevate blood pressure with regular consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~36 g/100 g) and high glycemic load per serving (~34)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this cake as an occasional indulgence only; a single 120 g slice delivers approximately 43 g of sugar and a glycemic load of ~34, which will cause a significant postprandial blood glucose rise and should be paired with physical activity or insulin adjustment as advised by a clinician.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~10.2 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.3 g/100 g), and high sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of this cake, as a single serving provides approximately 12.2 g of saturated fat and 0.36 g of trans fat, both of which raise LDL cholesterol and increase atherosclerotic risk; consumption should be limited to rare occasions within a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Gula Melaka Cake (Sliced) is a signature Singaporean dessert cake created and popularised by Sinpopo Coffee, a heritage-inspired café brand in Singapore. The cake is built around gula melaka — a dark, aromatic palm sugar derived from the sap of coconut or arenga palms, widely used in Southeast Asian and Peranakan cooking. The cake consists of multiple layers of soft, moist sponge infused with gula melaka syrup, sandwiched and coated with a rich gula melaka fudge filling. The result is a deeply caramel-like, smoky-sweet flavour profile with a tender, slightly dense crumb and a luscious, sticky fudge texture. A single slice weighs approximately 120 g and is priced at SGD 12.35 at Sinpopo Coffee's Funan outlet. Nutritionally, the cake is energy-dense at approximately 390 kcal per 100 g, with high sugar (~36 g/100 g) and saturated fat (~10.2 g/100 g) content. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low category), moderated by the lower GI of gula melaka relative to refined white sugar and the fat content slowing gastric emptying; however, the glycemic load per serving is high (~34) due to the large carbohydrate portion. The cake is vegetarian and halal-suitable but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs. It is not appropriate for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. Culturally, the Gula Melaka Cake represents a modern Singaporean innovation that elevates a traditional Southeast Asian sweetener into a contemporary café dessert format, bridging Peranakan culinary heritage with modern patisserie technique.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of gula melaka cake?
Is gula melaka cake suitable for diabetics?
What does gula melaka taste like?
Where can I buy gula melaka cake in Singapore?
What is the GI of gula melaka cake?
Is gula melaka healthier than sugar?
What is the difference between gula melaka and palm sugar?

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional laboratory data or approved-source entry exists for Gula Melaka Cake. Macronutrient values are estimated by adapting USDA SR Legacy data for comparable layered butter/fudge cake products and adjusting for gula melaka's known composition. GI is an analogous estimate anchored to University of Sydney GI Database values for butter cake and published gula melaka GI data. Confidence is medium rather than low because the estimation methodology is transparent, multi-source, and internally consistent.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogues (layered butter/fudge cake) adjusted for gula melaka composition; laboratory analysis of the actual Sinpopo product is required to achieve high confidence. 2. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero: baked butter/fudge cake contains no meaningful vitamin C; heat destruction during baking eliminates any residual ascorbic acid from ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy cake analogues. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cake analogue source used for estimation. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cake analogue source used for estimation. 5. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; the actual GI of this specific product may differ. 6. Trans fat value of 0.3 g/100 g is estimated based on typical commercial cake butter content; actual value may be lower if the product uses trans-fat-free butter. 7. Halal status is assumed based on typical ingredients; formal halal certification status of Sinpopo Coffee should be verified independently. 8. Price SGD 12.35 per slice preserved from field survey data source record (GD003042).
